segments.text: Call now, if there be any that will answer thee; and to which of the saints wilt thou turn?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For wrath killeth the foolish man, and envy slayeth the silly one.
I have seen the foolish taking root: but suddenly I cursed his habitation.
His children are far from safety, and they are crushed in the gate, neither is there any to deliver them.
Whose harvest the hungry eateth up, and taketh it even out of the thorns, and the robber swalloweth up their substance.
Although affliction cometh not forth of the dust, neither doth trouble spring out of the ground;
Yet man is born unto trouble, as the sparks fly upward.

footnotes: sparks…: Heb. the sons of the burning coal lift up to fly

segments.text: I would seek unto God, and unto God would I commit my cause:
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Which doeth great things and unsearchable; marvellous things without number:
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: unsearchable: Heb. there is no search
without…: Heb. till there be no number

segments.text: Who giveth rain upon the earth, and sendeth waters upon the fields:
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: To set up on high those that be low; that those which mourn may be exalted to safety.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He disappointeth the devices of the crafty, so that their hands cannot perform their enterprise.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He taketh the wise in their own craftiness: and the counsel of the froward is carried headlong.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They meet with darkness in the daytime, and grope in the noonday as in the night.
But he saveth the poor from the sword, from their mouth, and from the hand of the mighty.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: So the poor hath hope, and iniquity stoppeth her mouth.
Behold, happy is the man whom God correcteth: therefore despise not thou the chastening of the Almighty:
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: For he maketh sore, and bindeth up: he woundeth, and his hands make whole.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: He shall deliver thee in six troubles: yea, in seven there shall no evil touch thee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: In famine he shall redeem thee from death: and in war from the power of the sword.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thou shalt be hid from the scourge of the tongue: neither shalt thou be afraid of destruction when it cometh.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: from…: or, when the tongue scourgeth

segments.text: At destruction and famine thou shalt laugh: neither shalt thou be afraid of the beasts of the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: For thou shalt be in league with the stones of the field: and the beasts of the field shall be at peace with thee.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: And thou shalt know that thy tabernacle shall be in peace; and thou shalt visit thy habitation, and shalt not sin.
segments.speaker: God

footnotes: thy tabernacle…: or, peace is thy tabernacle

segments.text: Thou shalt know also that thy seed shall be great, and thine offspring as the grass of the earth.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Thou shalt come to thy grave in a full age, like as a shock of corn cometh in in his season.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Lo this, we have searched it, so it is; hear it, and know thou it for thy good.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz
